[2021-06-11 20:54] Deleted User (Deleted User): I don't care about exceptions, the point is I learn those as I play
[2021-06-11 20:54] crash_overlord: It's not the "perfect" approach if you're trying to be the best prismata player, but a good guideline I always used was thinking more about internal strategical synergy and almost never considering "how good is this unit".

So I'd generally be thinking along the lines of "I can go for unit 1, 2 and 5 together or I can go for unit 3, 4, 6 and maybe 7 if opponent does unit 8 ever".
I would almost never say "Oh, unit 1 is the strongest unit. How can I make sure I use unit 1 here."

Overall this works better imo than thinking about unit efficiency or tierlists for prismata. However, there are a few units where that 2nd approach becomes relevant, and those units tend to be (90% of the time I'd wager) absorb units (Centurion, defensegrid, xaetron, doomedwall, energy matrix etc.)

There's a few extra considerations like zemora/savior/wincer/tia as the big extremes, but you'll get used to those with experience playing the actual game.

[2021-06-11 21:27] crash_overlord: Ok so, it can be better than 5ish units of the total 80 or so?
[2021-06-11 21:28] crash_overlord: Maybe even better than 10 or 15 of all units when perfectly used compared to meh or average case use of the others?
Wow, that's totally not one of the worst units then.
[2021-06-11 21:29] crash_overlord: When looked at from best possible case scenario, rhino is at best an average unit. If that.
[2021-06-11 21:31] crash_overlord: So then, what would we consider "some of the worst units" in the game? The literal only worst unit? 
I'd argue it's a subsection of the units within the lower half of the average. I.e. the units that are on average, slightly more bad than the average bad unit.

I'd also argue that rhino most definitely falls into the "On average, worse than the average bad unit". I.e., bottom 25 percentile. And that's if we're being nice imo.

[2021-06-11 22:30] Deleted User (Deleted User): Blood Phage looks so strong
[2021-06-11 22:31] megasupp (mega-supp): tarsier+wild drone in one, but wild drone is untargetable
[2021-06-11 22:31] Deleted User (Deleted User): It's a rush card that builds economy
[2021-06-11 22:31] megasupp (mega-supp): also a very useful click
[2021-06-11 22:32] megasupp (mega-supp): buying a tarsier and a wild drone is the same as buying bloodphage
[2021-06-11 22:32] megasupp (mega-supp): except bloodfage can't be sniped and has a very useful click
[2021-06-11 22:33] amalloy: @Deleted User pretty much nothing that takes an extra turn to build is a rush card. just like tarsier, a turn is a long time
[2021-06-11 22:33] amalloy: it's good as a first attacker
[2021-06-11 22:33] megasupp (mega-supp): scorchilla into tarsiers into immolites sometimes just wins
[2021-06-11 22:34] Deleted User (Deleted User): Oh yeah Immolite is weird
[2021-06-11 22:34] Deleted User (Deleted User): On one hand seems great but on another seems not
[2021-06-11 22:34] amalloy: is that a rush? i feel like it's the opposite: delaying your attack so you don't waste any on absorb
[2021-06-11 22:34] megasupp (mega-supp): immolite in a vacuum is so sligthly more efficient than tarsier that it's negligible
[2021-06-11 22:35] Deleted User (Deleted User): I mean I don't mean rush rush
[2021-06-11 22:35] Deleted User (Deleted User): And wow I get econ for it
[2021-06-11 22:36] megasupp (mega-supp): I mean it kinda is a rush, because you are buying attack early
[2021-06-11 22:36] megasupp (mega-supp): rush is less about  when you actually get the attack it's how early do you start building it
[2021-06-11 22:37] amalloy: it's still buying your first attacker t4. which is on the early side, but fastimus is a pretty normal "rush" that's a turn faster, and even t2 attackers happen with smorcus
[2021-06-11 22:39] Deleted User (Deleted User): Fastimus sounds like a low tier harry potter spell
[2021-06-11 22:41] Deleted User (Deleted User): Bloodrager seems middle of pack
[2021-06-11 22:41] amalloy: the spell used to anesthetize tarsiers so you can remove their pain receptors
[2021-06-11 22:41] Deleted User (Deleted User): Good attack card but kinda hard to buy
[2021-06-11 22:41] Deleted User (Deleted User): And then you nerf your attack to get more
[2021-06-11 22:42] amalloy: @Deleted User absorb is a hugely important mechanic in prismata. your first few attackers are "useless" because your opponent's wall absorbs the attack
[2021-06-11 22:42] amalloy: blood rager is in a category of units that let you make use of your first few attack, by reinvesting it into something else. it's good (great?) when bought in that role, and pretty bad otherwise
[2021-06-11 22:42] Deleted User (Deleted User): I've heard defense wins in Prismata
[2021-06-11 22:43] Deleted User (Deleted User): Eventually you run out of walls
[2021-06-11 22:43] amalloy: you have to attack eventually, because attackers are more efficient en masse than econ
[2021-06-11 22:43] amalloy: but a small number of attackers is worse than none, because you had to take gold away from econ in order to buy them, and they get cancelled by just one wall
[2021-06-11 22:44] amalloy: so the better defense in the random set is, the longer you can afford to put off buying attackers to reinvest in economy
[2021-06-11 22:44] Deleted User (Deleted User): Longer builds means more overwhelming attack
[2021-06-11 22:45] jamberine: defense is op in that the best absorber is usually not skippable
[2021-06-11 22:45] jamberine: absorbers are very very good
[2021-06-11 22:46] Deleted User (Deleted User): Which can't absorb anything
[2021-06-11 22:47] jamberine: just to make sure are you familiar with absorb
[2021-06-11 22:48] Deleted User (Deleted User): I'm going to just start trashing aegis every chance I get
[2021-06-11 22:48] awaclus: aegis is good if you can make it fit into your build reasonably conveniently, it just isn't an absorber
[2021-06-11 22:48] Deleted User (Deleted User): Wow Bombarder looks nutty
